articles.title: ALL THREE
articles.summary: Marketing moves too fast to get bogged down by spreadsheets, email threads, and approvals. Your team deserves a work management platform built for the way marketers get things done: Asana [4].

articles.title: ➡️Try Asana free — no credit card required
ARE SOCIAL PLATFORM SUBSCRIPTIONS BECOMING MORE ACCEPTED?
articles.summary: Social platforms are moving toward paid add-ons, not full subscriptions. Meta Verified, LinkedIn Premium, and YouTube Premium all gate extra features while keeping core access free. Ads still drive most revenue, so platforms cannot force everyone to pay. Instead, they push optional upgrades for new income and bot control. As AI tools normalize subscriptions, users may accept paying for extras. However, reach still matters most, so social apps stick to hybrid models rather than fully paid access.

articles.title: RESEARCH SHOWS GENAI ADS PERFORM JUST AS WELL AS HUMAN-MADE CONTENT
articles.summary: A large-scale field study of more than 500 million impressions found AI-generated ads deliver click-through rates comparable to human-made creative when performance variables are controlled. Ads performed best when AI visuals avoided obvious synthetic cues and instead emphasized human elements, particularly clear human faces. AI creative maintained downstream conversion performance while dramatically improving speed and scale of production.

articles.title: WHY MOST ACTIVATION METRICS DON'T PREDICT WHO WILL STAY
articles.summary: Most activation metrics fail because they track steps or engagement, not whether users reach meaningful value. Signups, onboarding, or session length can look good while users drop off or never convert. True activation happens when users experience early and sustained value. Measuring first value and core value moments shows who is likely to stay. Focus on behaviors that drive long-term subscribers.

articles.title: AEO: OPTIMIZE FOR THE FOLLOW UP
articles.summary: Traditional SEO optimized for single queries and single clicks, but AI search turns discovery into a multi-step dialogue driven by follow-up questions. In this environment, content that answers only the first question quickly loses relevance as the model pulls subsequent answers from competitors. The recommendation is to design content around full question sequences, using customer support data and behavioral signals to predict what users ask next. Internal linking should guide that flow so both humans and LLMs see your site as complete across the journey.

articles.title: MODULR EMAIL WRITER
articles.summary: This tool scores email copy and shows how to improve it so messages are more engaging and conversion‑focused. It helps you check whether your emails are “boring” and suggests fixes based on the MODULR Method.

articles.title: ADS DECODED PODCAST BY GOOGLE ADS (YOUTUBE PLAYLIST)
articles.summary: The Ads Decoded Podcast connects advertisers with the teams building Google Ads. Hosted by Ginny Marvin, it delivers candid conversations that answer marketers' top questions.

articles.title: TRUST-BUILDING ADVERTISING WORKS
articles.summary: Trust-building advertising strongly drives business growth and brand performance for for-profit campaigns. New research from the IPA Effectiveness Databank shows that 93% of for-profit campaigns that deliver very large trust gains also deliver very large business effects, and top trust-building campaigns are 41% more likely to generate major commercial results than the average. These top campaigns are more than twice as likely to drive large gains in brand quality and loyalty and report +65% more very large business effects overall. Effectiveness increases when trust is a formal objective. The strongest trust-building campaigns typically use more channels at 10.7 versus 9.9, and split budgets 60% brand and 40% activation.

articles.title: WHY AI OUTPUT FAILS BEFORE GENERATION EVER BEGINS
articles.summary: AI content fails upstream, before generation begins. When buyer understanding lives in loose notes or long documents or generic problem lists, the system has nothing stable to reason against. So it averages vague inputs and produces fluent but unfocused output that drifts over time. Better prompts do not fix this. Stronger models only hide it. The real solution is structured context plus clear buyer narratives and defined constraints, so AI has a reference point instead of guessing.

articles.title: 4 GTM TRENDS FOR AI STARTUPS TO WATCH IN 2026
articles.summary: AI startups are redesigning GTM around proof, not promise, with pre-sales teams responsible for demonstrating value through structured pilots and ROI reporting. Buyers increasingly reject vague productivity claims in favor of cost-based savings that justify spend against headcount. With low barriers to product entry, early brand authority is becoming a primary growth lever through founder-led visibility and education. Discovery is shifting toward conversational AI, requiring content built for LLM prompts and neutral explanations rather than traditional SEO tactics.

articles.title: ZERO‑CLICK SEARCH IS HERE — WHAT IT MEANS FOR AD MEASUREMENT
articles.summary: Zero-click search is weakening site visits as a measurement signal, while organic branded search still signals consumer intent.

articles.title: META POSTS 25% REVENUE GROWTH QUARTER IN 2025, AS IMPRESSIONS AND AD PRICES SURGE
articles.summary: Meta delivered strong revenue growth by using AI to drive higher ad impressions and prices across its massive user base.
